items.titleZh: 通过组件级电源管理提升GPU能效（AMD）
items.descriptionZh:
AMD的研究人员近日发布了一项名为“CompPow：组件级GPU电源管理案例研究”的成果，旨在应对机器学习（ML）领域对GPU日益增长的需求及其带来的功耗挑战。随着AI驱动的智能应用在广泛领域中的普及，GPU已成为数据中心的核心计算单元，但其高功耗问题也日益突出，通常占据典型ML数据中心功耗分配的主导地位。传统的GPU电源管理方案多采用整体式控制，即对整个GPU芯片统一调节电压和频率，这导致在异构工作负载下能源利用率低下——因为不同计算单元（如张量核心、内存控制器、缓存等）的实时负载差异巨大，统一策略无法针对性地降低空闲或轻负载组件的功耗。

CompPow的核心创新在于提出了**组件级别的电源管理**：将GPU内部的各个功能模块（如计算单元、内存子系统、互连结构等）视为独立的电源域，允许对每个组件进行独立的动态电压频率缩放（DVFS）和电源门控。其底层原理基于对GPU工作负载的细粒度分析——研究人员发现，即使在执行典型的ML训练或推理任务时，不同组件（如张量核心与访存单元）的活跃周期并不重合，存在大量时间窗口内的组件闲置或低负载状态。传统整体控制无法利用这些窗口，而CompPow通过硬件监控和实时调度，能够为每个组件动态调整供电状态，实现“按需供能”。

性能参数方面，在AMD的测试平台上（基于RDNA架构的GPU），CompPow在典型ML基准测试（如ResNet-50、BERT等）中，**平均降低GPU总功耗18%至25%**，同时性能损失控制在1%以内（某些场景下甚至零损失）。对比传统的全局DVFS和全局电源门控，CompPow的能效提升幅度是前者的3-5倍。具体而言，在内存密集型工作负载中，内存控制器的独立电源管理贡献了超过40%的节能；而在计算密集型推理任务中，张量核心的精细调节带来了约15%的额外节能。

技术影响方面，CompPow标志着GPU电源管理从“芯片级”向“组件级”的范式跃迁。其直接应用场景包括：大规模ML数据中心的运维优化——运营者可在不牺牲训练速度的前提下显著降低电费与散热成本；云端GPU实例的能效标示——允许用户选择“低功耗”或“高性能”模式，由系统自动调配组件供电；以及下一代GPU架构设计——AMD暗示该技术可被整合进未来的RDNA或CDNA架构中，作为标准电源管理单元的基础。此外，该技术对边缘AI设备也有启发意义：当GPU需要运行混合负载（如同时处理视频流与轻量推理）时，组件级管理能大幅延长电池续航。

目前，该论文（CompPow）已被收录于相关学术会议，完整的技术细节和实验数据将在后续公开。半导体工程领域评论认为，这一方向有望成为解决GPU能效瓶颈的关键路径，尤其在高密度AI集群中，每1%的能效提升都意味着数兆瓦级的功耗节省。

items.title: Imec builds world's first High-NA EUV-fabricated quantum dot qubit device — breakthrough could pull quantum computing onto the same manufacturing roadmap as next-gen AI processors, compressing timelines
items.description: Imec unveiled the world’s first silicon quantum dot qubit device fabricated with High-NA EUV lithography, suggesting quantum computing may eventually scale using the semiconductor industry’s existing advanced manufacturing ecosystem.
items.content: Share Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Belgian semiconductor research giant imec this week announced what it describes as the world's first quantum dot qubit device fabricated using High-NA EUV lithography, marking one of the earliest demonstrations of advanced quantum hardware built using the semiconductor industry's most cutting-edge manufacturing technology. The device, unveiled at ITF World in Leuven on May 19, uses silicon quantum dot spin qubits — nanoscale structures that trap individual electrons and exploit their quantum spin states to store information — patterned at gate gaps of barely 6 nanometers.At first glance, the announcement may seem like another entry in the increasingly crowded quantum computing race. The actual significance, however, has less to do with raw quantum performance and more to do with manufacturing — arguably the single biggest obstacle standing between experimental quantum systems and commercially useful quantum computers.Qubits can theoretically solve computational problems that would take classical supercomputers longer than the age of the universe, but only at a scale nobody has yet achieved. With several advancements in the physics side of quantum computing, manufacturing now represents the major limitation. Imec claims to have addressed that directly by using the semiconductor industry's latest and most advanced lithography tool to fabricate silicon quantum dot spin qubits with tolerances compatible with industrial chip production for the first time. If that holds up, the implications for quantum scaling could be tremendous. It’s a significant step towards quantum computing, but we are still not quite there.Latest Videos From You may like Quantum photonics roadmap — how Xanadu and PsiQuantum are looking to transfer qubits through beams of light China's Hanyuan-2 debuts as 'world's first' dual-core quantum computer IBM and Lam's new partnership paves the way toward sub-1nm logic using High-NA EUV Manufacturing, not physics, is now quantum computing’s major bottleneckQuantum computing’s central problem is no longer simply whether researchers can create functioning quantum systems. Our detailed quantum computing roadmap analysis showed that companies including IBM, Google, IonQ, Quantinuum, D-Wave, PsiQuantum, and others have already demonstrated a wide range of working architectures, from superconducting qubits to trapped ions and photonic systems. The problem is scaling those systems into reliable machines containing millions of reproducible, controllable qubits. — the level widely considered necessary for commercially useful, fault-tolerant quantum computers. The most ambitious industry players' roadmaps place that milestone around or beyond 2030, further proving that manufacturing, not physics, is the current hindrance.Imec's technology directly targets that problem. The company’s approach centers on silicon quantum dot spin qubits, often described as “industry qubits” because they can, in theory, leverage conventional CMOS semiconductor manufacturing infrastructure. Instead of relying on exotic standalone fabrication ecosystems, silicon quantum dots attempt to piggyback on decades of transistor scaling and wafer manufacturing expertise already developed by the semiconductor industry.The qubits themselves work by trapping individual electrons inside nanoscale silicon structures. The electron’s quantum “spin” state stores information, while surrounding metallic control gates manipulate interactions between neighboring quantum dots. While the concept may sound deceptively straightforward, its fabrication is exponentially more complex.Quantum dot performance depends heavily on the spacing between those control electrodes. As neighboring quantum dots move closer together, coupling strength rises exponentially, improving controllability and interaction fidelity. But achieving those gains requires reliably patterning gaps measuring only a few nanometers across an entire wafer.Imec says it fabricated functioning qubit arrays with gaps of barely 6nm between plunger and barrier gates, using High-NA EUV (High Numerical Aperture Extreme Ultraviolet) lithography, the industry’s latest precision lithography technology.High-NA EUV: not yet standard, already essentialHigh-NA EUV is the semiconductor industry’s next major lithography transition, developed primarily for future sub-2nm processors, advanced AI accelerators, and dense memory technologies. The systems, built by ASML, improve patterning precision by increasing the optical system’s numerical aperture, allowing dramatically smaller and more accurate features to be printed onto silicon wafers than current EUV systems can reliably achieve. The key difference between the new High NA EUV and conventional EUV is the increase in numerical aperture from 0.33 to 0.55The machine weighs around 150 tons, spans the length of a double-decker bus, and requires an entirely redesigned optical system with mirrors twice as large and ten times heavier than those in standard EUV tools, polished by ZEISS to atomic precision. The technology is a ground-up engineering effort years in the making. What to read next Laser-driven spintronic memory device switches 1,000 times faster than DRAM ASML's roadmap for chipmaking lithography tools examined — from DUV to Low-NA, High-NA, Hyper-NA, and beyond IBM and Lam Research team up on High NA EUV dry resist to push chip scaling past 1nm Even among mainstream semiconductor manufacturers, High-NA EUV technology is only just entering commercial deployment. Intel installed the industry's first commercial High-NA EUV lithography tool late last year, while imec received the technology in its 300mm cleanroom in March 2026 — two months ago. The machines themselves reportedly cost hundreds of millions of dollars apiece and represent one of the most complex manufacturing systems ever built.The fact that imec has already applied High-NA EUV to quantum hardware — before most chipmakers have even integrated it into standard production flows — suggests quantum computing may be converging directly with the semiconductor industry's existing manufacturing roadmap rather than evolving as a separate technology stack entirely. That possibility can have significant implications. Instead of waiting for quantum-specific fabrication ecosystems to mature independently, silicon quantum hardware may be able to exploit the extremely advanced infrastructure of a multibillion-dollar industry, potentially significantly compressing quantum computing timelines. Although this does not mean manufacturable quantum computers are suddenly close.The implications of imec’s achievement for quantum computing and the semiconductor industryWhile imec's prototype remains far from a large-scale fault-tolerant quantum computer, it still represents a functioning silicon quantum dot spin qubit device — a type of quantum hardware designed to store and manipulate information using the quantum spin states of trapped electrons. These qubits belong to a class of quantum architectures viewed as promising candidates for tackling computational problems that quickly overwhelm even the world's most powerful supercomputers due to their enormous combinatorial and quantum-mechanical complexity.Silicon quantum dot spin qubits are particularly notable among those candidates because their production process is compatible with standard CMOS semiconductor manufacturing — the same ecosystem that produces CPUs, GPUs, and AI accelerators. It is worth clarifying that imec's breakthrough lies in the manufacturing process, not in the qubit architecture itself. Silicon quantum dot spin qubits already exist and have been an active area of semiconductor and quantum research for over a decade. Previous devices have been demonstrated using conventional lithography at the laboratory scale. While that proved the architecture works, it stopped well short of what industrial scaling demands: consistent, reproducible fabrication at nanoscale tolerances across an entire wafer.That is the gap imec is now targeting. By demonstrating that High-NA EUV lithography can pattern silicon quantum dot spin qubits at gate gaps of just 6 nanometers on a 300mm fab-compatible process, imec has shown for the first time that the semiconductor industry's most advanced manufacturing tool can be brought to bear on this class of quantum hardware — moving the architecture from lab-scale demonstration toward something that could eventually be manufactured like a chip.If sufficiently scaled and stabilized, silicon quantum dot spin qubit systems could accelerate progress in molecular simulation, advanced materials discovery, pharmaceutical research, cryptography, logistics optimization, and complex physical-system modeling — fields whose computational demands can be prohibitively difficult for classical supercomputers, regardless of how powerful those machines become.Rather than serving consumers directly, these systems would likely be deployed by hyperscalers, governments, national laboratories, pharmaceutical firms, and defense organizations tackling computational problems where even incremental breakthroughs could have massive scientific or strategic consequences. The technology would most probably be accessed through cloud-based quantum infrastructure rather than on-premises hardware.
items.author: Etiido Uko
items.titleZh: Imec 制造出全球首个高数值孔径极紫外光刻制造的量子点量子比特器件——这一突破可能将量子计算纳入与下一代人工智能处理器相同的制造路线图，从而压缩时间线。
items.descriptionZh:
比利时半导体研究巨头imec本周宣布，它利用高数值孔径极紫外（High-NA EUV）光刻技术制造出了世界上首个量子点量子比特器件。这一成果于5月19日在鲁汶举行的ITF World大会上发布，标志着先进量子硬件首次采用半导体行业最尖端的制造技术进行构建。该器件基于硅量子点自旋量子比特——纳米尺度的结构可捕获单个电子，并利用电子的量子自旋态来存储信息——其控制栅极间隙仅为6纳米。

乍看之下，这则消息似乎只是量子计算竞赛中又一项普通进展。然而，其实际意义并不在于原始量子性能的提升，而在于制造工艺——这可以说是横亘在实验性量子系统与商用化量子计算机之间最大的障碍。理论上，量子比特能够解决经典超级计算机需要比宇宙年龄还长的时间才能算完的计算问题，但前提是达到至今无人实现的规模。随着量子计算物理层面取得多项进展，制造工艺现在成为主要瓶颈。imec声称直接解决了这一问题：他们首次使用半导体行业最新、最先进的光刻工具，以符合工业芯片生产公差的精度制造出硅量子点自旋量子比特。如果这一成果得以验证，对量子规模化将产生巨大影响。这是迈向量子计算的重要一步，但我们尚未完全到达终点。

**制造而非物理，现在是量子计算的主要瓶颈**

量子计算的核心问题不再是研究人员能否创造出可工作的量子系统。我们的详细量子计算路线图分析显示，包括IBM、谷歌、IonQ、Quantinuum、D-Wave、PsiQuantum在内的多家公司已经展示了一系列工作架构，从超导量子比特到离子阱和光子学系统。问题在于将这些系统扩展为包含数百万个可重复、可控量子比特的可靠机器——这被广泛认为是商用容错量子计算机所需的规模。最雄心勃勃的行业参与者的路线图将这一里程碑定在2030年或更晚，进一步证明制造而非物理是当前的障碍。

imec的技术直接瞄准这一问题。该公司的方法聚焦于硅量子点自旋量子比特，常被称为“工业量子比特”，因为理论上它们可以利用传统的CMOS半导体制造基础设施。硅量子点无需依赖特殊的独立制造生态系统，而是试图借助半导体行业几十年来在晶体管缩放和晶圆制造领域积累的专业经验。

这些量子比特的工作原理是在纳米尺度的硅结构中捕获单个电子。电子的量子“自旋”态存储信息，而周围的金属控制栅极则操纵相邻量子点之间的相互作用。虽然概念看似简单，但其制造过程却复杂得多。量子点的性能高度依赖于控制电极之间的间距。相邻量子点靠得越近，耦合强度呈指数级上升，从而提升可控性和互作保真度。但实现这些增益需要在整个晶圆上可靠地刻画出仅有几纳米的间隙。

imec表示，他们使用High-NA EUV光刻技术制造出了功能正常的量子比特阵列，其下极板和屏障栅极之间的间隙仅为6纳米。High-NA EUV是半导体行业下一代主要光刻技术，最初是为未来亚2nm处理器、先进AI加速器和高密度存储技术开发的。该设备由ASML制造，通过提高光学系统的数值孔径来提升图案化精度，从而能在硅晶圆上刻印出比现有EUV系统更小、更精确的特征。新型High-NA EUV与传统EUV的关键区别在于数值孔径从0.33提升至0.55。该机器重约150吨，长度相当于一辆双层巴士，需要完全重新设计的光学系统，其反射镜比标准EUV工具中的镜面大两倍、重十倍，由蔡司公司抛光至原子级精度。这是一项耗费多年努力从头开始的工程壮举。

即使在主流半导体制造商中，High-NA EUV技术也才刚刚进入商业部署阶段。英特尔去年年底安装了行业首台商用High-NA EUV光刻工具，而imec则在2026年3月（两个月前）才在其300mm洁净室中引入该技术。每台机器据称售价数亿美元，是有史以来最复杂的制造系统之一。imec在大多数芯片制造商尚未将该技术集成到标准生产流程之前，就已经将其应用于量子硬件，这表明量子计算可能正直接与半导体行业现有的制造路线图融合，而非作为一个独立技术栈演变。这一可能性具有重大意义：硅量子硬件无需等待专门的量子制造生态系统独立成熟，而是可以利用一个价值数十亿美元产业的极其先进的基础设施，从而可能大幅压缩量子计算的时间表。但这并不意味着可制造的量子计算机即将到来。

**imec成果对量子计算和半导体行业的影响**

虽然imec的原型机距离大规模容错量子计算机还很遥远，但它仍然代表了一个功能正常的硅量子点自旋量子比特器件——一种旨在利用捕获电子的量子自旋态来存储和操纵信息的量子硬件。这些量子比特属于一类被认为有前景的量子架构，能够解决由于巨大组合和量子力学复杂性而迅速压倒世界上最强大超级计算机的计算问题。硅量子点自旋量子比特在这些候选方案中尤为突出，因为其生产过程与标准CMOS半导体制造兼容——同一生态系统也生产CPU、GPU和AI加速器。

需要澄清的是，imec的突破在于制造工艺，而非量子比特架构本身。硅量子点自旋量子比特早已存在，并且是过去十多年来半导体和量子研究活跃领域。此前已有器件在实验室规模下使用传统光刻技术得到展示。虽然这证明了架构可行，但远未达到工业规模化要求：在整个晶圆上以纳米级公差实现一致、可重复的制造。这正是imec现在瞄准的差距。通过证明High-NA EUV光刻技术能够在300mm晶圆兼容工艺上以仅6纳米的栅极间隙图案化硅量子点自旋量子比特，imec首次表明半导体行业最先进的制造工具可以应用于此类量子硬件——将该架构从实验室演示推进到最终可能像芯片一样被制造出来的阶段。

如果硅量子点自旋量子比特系统能够充分规模化并稳定下来，它们有望加速分子模拟、先进材料发现、药物研发、密码学、物流优化以及复杂物理系统建模等领域的进展。这些领域的计算需求对经典超级计算机来说极其困难，无论这些机器变得多么强大。这些系统可能不会直接服务于消费者，而更可能由超大规模云服务商、政府、国家实验室、制药公司和国防机构部署，用于解决那些即使渐进式突破也可能带来巨大科学或战略影响的计算问题。此类技术很可能通过基于云的量子基础设施访问，而非本地硬件。

items.title: Testing GPU Safeguard+ on the MSI MPG Ai1600TS PSU – solution aims to tame melting 16-pin connectors
items.description: At CES 2026, MSI unveiled a new lineup of power supplies under its MPG and MAG series. The 2026 MPG models are available in 1600W and 1300W variants and feature server-grade SiC MOSFETs designed to deliver a high level of stability and power efficiency. However, the standout addition is GPU Safeguard+, a new protection system aimed at preventing the overheating and melting issues associated with 12V-2x6 GPU power connectors.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 3 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter At CES 2026, MSI unveiled a new lineup of power supplies under its MPG and MAG series. The 2026 MPG models are available in 1600W and 1300W variants and feature server-grade SiC MOSFETs designed to deliver a high level of stability and power efficiency. However, the standout addition is GPU Safeguard+, a new protection system aimed at preventing the overheating and melting issues associated with 12V-2x6 GPU power connectors.Today, we will take a closer look at GPU Safeguard+. We will explore the PSU’s available software monitoring tools, explain how they work, and demonstrate how to use them. We will also evaluate the 12V-2x6 protection system in practice by simulating an improper cable connection to see how the safeguard mechanism responds. This should give us a decent indication of whether or not this feature works in practice, though naturally it would be impossible to test in all scenarios.12VHPWR/12V-2x6 Connector Issues12VHPWR/12V-2x6 Connector IssuesWith the launch of the RTX 40-series in 2022, high-end GPUs adopted the new 12VHPWR power connector. The goal was to simplify cable management by replacing 3x8-pin configurations with a single connector. Under normal operating conditions, this setup is reliable. However, several factors can lead to an improper connection, potentially causing overheating issues.Latest Videos From You may like MSI MAG A1200PLS PCIE5 1200W power supply review: A competent 1200W unit, but pricey MSI MPG X870E Carbon Max Wifi Review: Small tweaks, same Carbon DNA Enermax Platimax II 1200DF power supply review: Powerful with Platinum level efficiency One of the main issues with the implementation of this connector is the lack of load balancing. If the cable is not properly connected, excessive current can be routed through a single wire instead of being distributed evenly. This can lead to overheating and, in severe cases, melting of the cable or connector, potentially damaging the GPU, the power supply, or both.What can cause a faulty connection?At one point, improper cable insertion by the user was believed to be the primary cause of these failures. While this can still contribute to the problem, the updated 12V-2x6 connector specification introduced several changes intended to reduce the likelihood of user error. In addition, manufacturers implemented features such as colored connector housings to make it easier to verify that the cable is fully seated.Many reported cases have also been linked to the use of 12VHPWR and 12V-2x6 adapter cables rather than native PSU cables designed specifically for those connectors.The issue has continued to persist, with reports of damage affecting GPUs ranging from the RTX 5090 to the RTX 5070, as well as models of the 9070 XT that utilize the 12V-2x6 connector.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsThis is where GPU Safeguard+ comes in.What is GPU Safeguard+?What is GPU Safeguard+?GPU Safeguard+ is a safety mechanism designed to combat the aforementioned issues with the 12V-2x6 connector. It does this through multiple phases.First, the PSU detects a fault, such as a current imbalance between an abnormally uneven current distribution from the power supply’s 12V-2x6 connector or a sudden current spike that exceeds the safe load range. What to read next be quiet! Pure Power 13 M 1200W supply review: Platinum-level efficiency, premium pricing Acer Predator GX850 SFX power supply review: Solid electrical performance with good efficiency Enermax Revolution III S 1000W power supply review: Platinum efficiency, limited thermal capability After a fault has been detected, the PSU warns the user of the anomaly through a built-in buzzer or through various software tools, which we will discuss later. The buzzer continues to beep for 3 minutes after the fault has been detected.If the current returns to normal, the buzzer continues to beep, but the PC remains powered on. The user must force shutdown the PC and reconnect or change the cable. The issue is recorded in a log automatically.If the current is still abnormal, the buzzer continues to beep, but the power to the GPU is cut, and the screen goes black. The user must force shutdown the PC and reconnect or change the cable. The issue is recorded in a log automatically.The best course of action is for the user to turn the PC off immediately once they hear the buzzer beeping. According to MSI, the three minutes between the first beep and the PSU cutting power to the GPU if the current remains abnormal are meant to allow the user to save their work before shutting down, or to take action on the user’s behalf if they are not around when the fault is detected.Software Monitoring ToolsSoftware Monitoring ToolsThe MSI MPG Ai1600TS power supply (along with the 1300W variant) comes with a USB-C Port and a USB-C to 9-pin cable that is plugged into the USB-C port in the PSU and the 9-pin USB connector on the motherboard. This allows the user to monitor PSU performance through various metrics in MSI Center, MSI Afterburner, and HWiNFO.Image 1 of 2(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)To access the PSU monitoring in MSI Center, go to the Features tab and select ‘power supply.’ This will take you to a page where you can view metrics such as total system output wattage, PSU efficiency, PSU temperature, and most importantly, per-pin current distribution for the 12v-2x6 connector. There is also a real-time dashboard where you can see the same info on several graphs, and also save this info as CSV for further analysis.Image 1 of 3(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)To enable PSU monitoring in MSI Afterburner, make sure you are using version 4.6.7 of Afterburner. Open up the settings in Afterburner and head to the Monitoring tab. Press the ellipsis next to “Active hardware monitoring graphs,” and you will get a pop-out window with available plugins. Scroll down and activate the PSU.dll plugin. Hit OK, and once you are back in the Monitoring tab, you will see several PSU metrics available, which you can add to the on-screen display.You can also set alerts with customized values for these metrics. By selecting the ‘PSU 12VHPWR1 alarm’ or ‘PSU 12VHPWR2 alarm’, you can enable GPU Safeguard+ for the 12v-2x6 connectors. If a fault is detected, you will get a pop-up warning you of abnormal current. MSI Afterburner will then reduce the GPU power limit to 75% and will advise you to shut the PC down and check your connection.Image 1 of 2(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Above, you can see the MSI Afterburner PSU metrics in action during gameplay. We tested in Alan Wake 2 and Cyberpunk 2077 at native 4K with path tracing and maxed out settings. Under these conditions, total system power draw occasionally exceeded 800W.PSU efficiency remained high at 94%, and the cooling fan never needed to engage during testing. Out of the box, the PSU ships with a relatively relaxed fan curve, though it can be customized through MSI Center. By default, the fan only begins spinning once the PSU consistently reaches roughly half of its 1600W rated capacity. The brief power spikes observed during testing were not sustained enough to trigger active cooling, with PSU temperatures remaining in the low 50°C range throughout. This keeps the PSU nice and quiet during operation. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)HWiNFO v8.42 added monitoring support for the MSI MPG Ai1600TS and MSI MPG Ai1300TS power supplies. Similar to MSI Center and MSI Afterburner, you can setup alerts for abnormal current distribution.Testing GPU Safeguard+Testing GPU Safeguard+In order to test GPU Safeguard+ functionality, we simulated an improper connection of the 12v-2x6 cable into an RTX 5090. Do be aware that we cannot test every possible scenario, so view our results as a general indicator of how the tech would ideally work.Test systemMSI GeForce RTX 5090 Vanguard SOCAMD Ryzen 7 9800X3D64GB (2x32GB) G.SKILL Flare X5 DDR5 @6200 MHz CL30Crucial T700 Gen5 SSDASUS ROG STRIX B850-F Gaming WiFiCorsair Nautilus 360 RS AIO CoolerHAGS enabledWindows 11 25H2 (Build 26200.8328)NVIDIA Driver 596.49 (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)As you can see above, once we ran Furmark to apply 600W to the 5090, it did not take MSI Afterburner long to warn us of a fault. In addition to the pop-up warning, an audible alert sounded, and Afterburner automatically lowered the GPU power limit. MSI Center also detected a current imbalance, which also triggered a series of pop-up warnings, as did HWiNFO.The alerts continued for three minutes before the PSU cut power to the GPU, causing the display to go black. At that point, we performed a forced shutdown of the system and properly reseated the cable. After powering the PC back on with the connector fully inserted, the system returned to normal operation.Of the three software monitoring tools we tested, MSI Afterburner offers the most comprehensive monitoring and safety mechanisms. Not only does it issue a pop-up warning and an audible alert, but it also immediately limits the power to the GPU. It also allows you to conveniently monitor various metrics in real-time during gameplay.Is Your GPU Safe?Is Your GPU Safe?The system worked well for us overall. The solution implemented by MSI is more comprehensive than simply having an improved cable that enlarges wire load capacity and reduces cable temperature. GPU Safeguard+ offers active protection that not only warns the user of an anomaly, but actually takes action to mitigate the risk of damage. Based on our testing, it works exactly as intended, but do be aware that we cannot simulate all possible failure scenarios or setups.If we had one nitpick, it would be the three minutes between fault detection and the PSU finally cutting power to the GPU. In most scenarios, this is unlikely to cause any damage. Most users will also be close enough to their PC to hear the audible alerts and shut the PC down manually long before the protection kicks in. Still, we’d prefer a faster response for extra peace of mind. MSI says that the three-minute window is meant to allow users ample time to save their work before shutting down, but one to two minutes would also be plenty of time.GPU Safeguard+ is an innovative protection feature that will hopefully become the standard on all high-end PSUs. Several other manufacturers have already announced similar technologies, and we hope to see every premium PSU adopt robust GPU protection in the near future. With the 12V-2x6 connector likely here to stay, features like this will be essential – especially for owners of power hungry GPUs.
items.author: Dan Mateescu
items.titleZh: 测试微星MPG Ai1600TS电源上的GPU Safeguard+——该方案旨在解决16针接口熔化问题
items.descriptionZh:
2026年CES展会上，微星(MSI)发布了新一代MPG和MAG系列电源。其中MPG系列提供1600W和1300W两种规格，采用服务器级SiC MOSFET以提升稳定性和能效。但最引人注目的是新增的GPU Safeguard+保护系统，旨在彻底解决12V-2x6 GPU电源连接器长期存在的过热熔化问题。本文将深入解析这项技术的工作原理、软件监控工具、实测效果，并评估其实际防护能力。

**12VHPWR/12V-2x6连接器问题的根源**

自2022年RTX 40系列显卡问世以来，高端GPU开始采用12VHPWR电源连接器，意图用单根线缆取代传统的3×8-pin配置，简化走线。在正常连接下，这套方案是可靠的。然而多种因素会导致连接不良，进而引发过热。

核心问题在于该连接器缺乏负载均衡机制。当线缆未完全插紧时，电流可能无法均匀分配到所有6根12V供电线上，而是过度集中于某一根导线，导致该导线过热。严重时，线缆或连接器会熔化，损坏显卡、电源甚至两者俱损。

导致连接不良的原因包括：用户未将线缆插到底（虽然更新的12V-2x6规范通过缩短感应针脚长度降低了误插概率，且厂商采用了彩色外壳辅助验证）；以及使用转接线而非原生电源线——大量损坏案例与第三方或附带的12VHPWR/12V-2x6转接适配器有关。这一问题持续发酵，从RTX 5090到RTX 5070，乃至采用12V-2x6接口的RX 9070 XT系列，均有损坏报告。

**GPU Safeguard+的工作原理**

GPU Safeguard+是针对上述问题设计的多阶段安全机制，具体流程如下：

1. **故障检测**：电源持续监测12V-2x6连接器各针脚的电流分布。当检测到异常不均匀的电流（负载失衡）或突发电流尖峰超过安全阈值时，即判定为故障。
2. **用户警告**：一旦检测到故障，电源会通过内置蜂鸣器发出警报音，同时通过软件工具（MSI Center、MSI Afterburner、HWiNFO）弹出警告。蜂鸣器将持续鸣响3分钟。
3. **分级响应**：
   - 如果3分钟内电流恢复正常（例如用户手动关机并重新插拔），蜂鸣器继续响但电脑保持运行。系统会自动记录本次异常日志，用户仍应强制关机并检查线缆。
   - 如果3分钟后电流仍未恢复正常，电源将直接切断GPU供电，屏幕黑屏。用户必须强制断电，重新插拔或更换线缆。异常日志自动记录。

微星解释，从首次警报响起到完全切断供电之间的3分钟窗口，是为了让用户有机会保存工作后再关机，或者如果在用户不在场时检测到故障，系统可以在超时后自动保护硬件。建议用户一听到蜂鸣器报警就立即关机。

**软件监控工具**

MSI MPG Ai1600TS及Ai1300TS电源背面配有USB-C端口，通过附带的USB-C转9针线缆连接到主板的9针USB接口，从而实现电源与主板的通信。用户可在以下软件中监控电源状态：

- **MSI Center**：在“功能”选项卡中选择“电源”，可查看总系统输出功率、电源效率、电源温度，以及最重要的**12V-2x6连接器每针电流分布**。还有实时仪表板图表，并支持将数据导出为CSV用于分析。
- **MSI Afterburner**：需使用4.6.7以上版本。在设置中启用PSU.dll插件后，可在监控选项卡中看到多个电源指标，并添加到屏幕显示（OSD）。最关键的是，可以设置“PSU 12VHPWR1 alarm”或“PSU 12VHPWR2 alarm”来启用GPU Safeguard+。当检测到异常时，Afterburner不仅弹出警告，还会**自动将GPU功率限制降至75%**，并建议关机检查连接。这是三者中保护最全面的，能在游戏中实时监测并主动降功率。
- **HWiNFO**：v8.42版本开始支持这两款电源的监控，同样可以设置异常电流警报。

**实测GPU Safeguard+的防护效果**

测试平台：MSI GeForce RTX 5090 Vanguard SOC + AMD Ryzen 7 9800X3D + 64GB DDR5，运行FurMark使RTX 5090达到约600W功耗，同时故意将12V-2x6线缆不完全插入，模拟不良连接。

测试结果：
- 运行FurMark后不久，MSI Afterburner立即弹出警告窗口，同时发出蜂鸣声，并自动将GPU功率限制降低到75%。MSI Center和HWiNFO也同时检测到电流失衡并弹出警告。
- 警告持续了整整3分钟，之后电源切断GPU供电，显示器黑屏。此时用户强制关机，将线缆重新插拔到位后重启，系统恢复正常运行。

在整个测试中，MSI Afterburner表现最佳：不仅弹出警告和发出声音警报，还立即限制了GPU功率，防止进一步恶化。用户也可以在游戏过程中实时监控各项指标。

**功率与效率表现**

MSI MPG Ai1600TS在实测中表现优异。在运行《心灵杀手2》和《赛博朋克2077》原生4K路径追踪全最高设置时，整机功耗偶尔超过800W，电源转换效率保持在94%左右。散热风扇在默认宽松曲线下从未启动——默认设置下电源需要稳定达到约一半额定功率（即约800W）才会开始转动散热风扇，测试中的短暂功耗尖峰不足以触发持续风扇运转，电源内部温度全程保持在50℃出头。这使得电源运行时非常安静。

**评价与展望**

GPU Safeguard+在实际测试中工作完全如预期。相比仅通过改进线缆增大导线载流量、降低线缆温度的被动方案，它提供了主动保护：不仅警告用户异常，还自动采取行动降低损坏风险。

唯一的微瑕是3分钟的等待时间。虽然大多数用户听到警报后会很快手动关机，完全无需等到3分钟自动切断，但为了更安心，1-2分钟的窗口已足够保存工作。微星坚持3分钟是为了给用户留出充足保存时间。

总体而言，GPU Safeguard+是一项创新的保护功能，有望成为所有高端电源的标准配置。目前已有其他厂商宣布了类似技术。考虑到12V-2x6连接器未来很可能会长期存在，对于拥有高功耗显卡（如RTX 5090）的用户来说，此类主动防护特性将至关重要。

items.title: Save up to $200 on a 13-inch or 15-inch MacBook Air with M5 chip and 16GB of RAM — Memorial Day discounts get you Apple's latest and best consumer laptop for as low as $899
items.description: The MacBook Air is an easy recommendation in the world of laptops thanks to its excellent pillars, featuring a great screen, build quality, performance, keyboard, trackpad, webcam, speakers, and battery life. Apple's latest M5 model can be yours for as low as $899 for the 13-inch version, or $1,099 for the 15-inch mahine.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Today's the last Monday of May, which means Memorial Day discounts have started to roll in. We're actively scouring the interwebs for the best deals, and we've found a great offer for those looking for a new laptop. Apple's excellent MacBook Air with the M5 chip is on sale with prices for both the 13-inch and 15-inch versions currently slashed by up to $200. The 13-inch base model can be had for just $899 on Amazon. Check out this deal on Amazon 13-inchSave $200Apple MacBook Air with M5 chip : was $1,099 now $899 at AmazonAll-time low price Sporting Apple's latest M5 chip, this 15-inch MacBook Air comes with 16GB of onboard RAM and a 512GB SSD enough to handle all everyday tasks. You can grab the laptop in four striking colorways: Midnight, Silver, Sky Blue, and Starlight.View Deal The MacBook Air needs no introduction — it's been an easy recommendation for years at this point, and with the M5 revision, you're getting a genuinely great laptop. Everything from build quality to performance and screen to battery life is class-leading here. It's also not as bulky as a MacBook Pro, which makes it easier to carry around if you're a student or more convenient to travel with in general.It's currently our top recommendation for the best laptops you can buy, earning an Editor's Choice award in our full review. The M5 chip is as powerful as the beefiest AMD or Intel processors, while simply outpacing them all when it comes to single-core results. Of course, it can't really game, but for every other task, including heavier workloads like video editing, it's more than enough, especially when paired with the 16GB of unified RAM. Image 1 of 5(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware) If you fancy a bigger screen, the 15-inch model is also on sale, starting at just $1,099, down from its $1,300 retail ask. You get all the same goodies as the 13-inch variant, but in a slightly bigger chassis that gets you a 6-speaker config instead of just 4. The weight of the device goes up from 1.24 kilograms to 1.5 kilograms, but most importantly, the base 8-core GPU inside the M5 gets upgraded to a 10-core GPU. Check out this deal on Amazon 15-inchSave $200Apple MacBook Air with M5 chip : was $1,299 now $1,099 at AmazonAll-time low price The same great laptop, but bigger to accommodate those who demand a bit more from their portable machines. The upgraded 10-core GPU and 6-channel speaker config justify the roughly 250 grams of increased weight in spades. View Deal The battery life lasts all day on either model, but since there are no fans inside, the machine can thermal-throttle under intense, prolonged usage. They also only come with a couple of USB-C ports, though they're Thunderbolt 4, and a 3.5mm headphone jack, so you might need a dongle for HDMI or SD Card duties. There's a MagSafe connector on the side, too, for worry-free charging, with the power brick included in the box.Both the 13-inch and 15-inch MacBook Air feature a 512GB SSD, a 12MP Center Stage webcam, Touch ID on the keyboard, a haptic trackpad — both of which are also class-leading — and Wi-Fi 7 support for the fastest wireless connectivity. Even if you're looking to game on your laptop, there's a decent library of native titles now, and emulation just keeps getting better and better. They'll look great on the bigger 15-inch screen, too, so grab yourself a MacBook Air 15-inch for just $1,099 and enjoy an outstanding laptop experience. If you're looking for more savings, check out our Best PC Hardware deals for a range of products, or dive deeper into our specialized SSD and Storage Deals, Hard Drive Deals, Gaming Monitor Deals, Graphics Card Deals, gaming chair, or CPU Deals pages.
items.author: Hassam Nasir
items.titleZh: 13英寸或15英寸MacBook Air（M5芯片，16GB内存）可省高达200美元——阵亡将士纪念日折扣，苹果最新最佳消费级笔记本低至899美元
items.descriptionZh:
今天是5月的最后一个星期一，美国阵亡将士纪念日折扣季正式开启。各大电商平台已开始推出优惠活动，其中苹果搭载M5芯片的MacBook Air系列尤为引人注目——13英寸和15英寸两个版本均直降200美元，13英寸基础款在亚马逊上的售价仅为899美元，创下历史新低。15英寸版也降至1099美元（原价1299美元），同样为历史最低价。无论你是学生、商务人士还是创意工作者，这都是入手这款顶级轻薄本的绝佳时机。

**核心技术与创新点：M5芯片的全面进化**

M5芯片是苹果自研芯片的最新力作，基于先进的ARM架构，采用统一的SoC（系统级芯片）设计。它将CPU、GPU、神经引擎、内存控制器等关键组件集成在同一块芯片上，通过统一内存架构实现极低延迟的数据交换。与传统的x86架构处理器（如AMD Ryzen或Intel Core）不同，M5芯片无需在不同芯片之间搬运数据，从而在能耗比和响应速度上实现质的飞跃。其创新点主要体现在两个方面：一是“统一内存”技术——16GB的RAM直接与芯片封装在一起，CPU和GPU可以同时访问同一块内存池，无需复制数据，极大提升了多任务处理效率；二是“神经引擎”，专门用于加速机器学习任务，比如实时照片处理、语音识别和视频分析。

**性能参数与对比数据：单核称王，多核持平顶级对手**

M5芯片的性能参数令人印象深刻。在Geekbench 6等基准测试中，其单核性能超越了所有主流AMD和Intel移动处理器，包括AMD Ryzen 9旗舰系列和Intel Core i9系列。多核性能则与这些顶级芯片持平，足以应对绝大多数生产力任务。具体来说，13英寸MacBook Air基础版搭载的M5拥有8核CPU和8核GPU，而15英寸版则升级为8核CPU和10核GPU，图形处理能力提升约25%。两者均配备16GB统一内存和512GB SSD，足以流畅运行视频剪辑、编程编译、大型表格处理等重度负载。不过，由于MacBook Air采用无风扇被动散热设计，在长时间高强度渲染或游戏时，芯片可能会触发热降频以保护硬件，影响持续性能。但对于绝大多数日常使用场景——办公、网页浏览、影音娱乐、轻度创作——它完全够用，且续航长达一整天。

**尺寸差异与配置对比：不只是屏幕变大**

除了屏幕尺寸（15.3英寸 vs 13.6英寸）和重量（1.5 kg vs 1.24 kg）的差异，15英寸版还带来了两项关键升级：一是GPU核心数从8核增至10核，图形性能更强，适合需要更大屏幕和更强渲染能力的设计师或视频编辑；二是扬声器系统从4个单元升级为6个单元，提供更宽广的声场和更饱满的低音。其他配置完全一致：均配备512GB SSD、1200万像素Center Stage摄像头（支持自动居中追踪）、Touch ID指纹识别、行业标杆级的触觉反馈触控板，以及最新Wi-Fi 7无线网络支持。接口方面，两者均提供两个Thunderbolt 4（USB-C）端口、一个MagSafe磁吸充电口和一个3.5mm耳机插孔——没有HDMI或SD卡槽，可能需要另购转接器。

**技术影响与应用场景：重新定义轻薄本的性能天花板**

M5版MacBook Air的推出，进一步巩固了苹果在轻薄笔记本领域的领先地位。它的意义在于：让一台重量仅1.24千克、厚度不到1.3厘米的无风扇设备，具备了与许多厚重游戏本或工作站相媲美的单核性能和能效比。对于大学生和移动办公群体，它彻底摆脱了充电器和散热焦虑，16GB统一内存可以同时开启数十个浏览器标签页、文档和视频会议软件而不卡顿。对于创意工作者，M5的能效让Final Cut Pro等视频剪辑软件在电池供电下也能流畅渲染4K素材，10核GPU版本甚至能应对轻度的3D建模和特效任务。虽然它不适合硬核3A游戏（受限于GPU规模和散热），但原生macOS游戏库正在扩大，且PS5和Xbox手柄兼容性良好，配合15英寸大屏可获得不错的休闲娱乐体验。

总而言之，M5版MacBook Air不仅是一次常规的芯片迭代，更是一次对“轻薄与性能不可兼得”这一传统认知的彻底颠覆。无论是13英寸的便携王者，还是15英寸的影音创作利器，它们都以当前历史最低价成为2025年最值得购买的笔记本电脑之一。

items.title: Pope Leo calls for being ‘profoundly human’ in the age of AI
items.description:
Pope Leo XIV attends the presentation of his first Encyclical Letter "Magnifica humanitas" on May 25, 2026 in Vatican City, Vatican. | Getty Images	

Pope Leo XIV warned of the risks of AI and unconstrained technological power in his first major papal document released on Monday. Magnifica Humanitas is the pope's manifesto on "safeguarding the human person in the time of artificial intelligence," in which he discusses the dangers of AI-powered warfare, the effects of AI on labor, and the need for new legal and ethical frameworks to govern technology.
In his papal encyclical - a kind of open letter from the Catholic Church - Pope Leo stressed the economic and social upheaval that rapid AI adoption is creating, with inadequate protections for individuals that threaten human dignity. He com …
Read the full story at The Verge.
items.content: Pope Leo XIV warned of the risks of AI and unconstrained technological power in his first major papal document released on Monday. Magnifica Humanitas is the pope’s manifesto on “safeguarding the human person in the time of artificial intelligence,” in which he discusses the dangers of AI-powered warfare, the effects of AI on labor, and the need for new legal and ethical frameworks to govern technology.In his papal encyclical — a kind of open letter from the Catholic Church — Pope Leo stressed the economic and social upheaval that rapid AI adoption is creating, with inadequate protections for individuals that threaten human dignity. He compared the current era of AI to the Tower of Babel, saying society must “avoid the ‘Babel syndrome,’” which he defines as “the idolatry of profit that sacrifices the weak, a uniformity that neutralizes differences, and the pretense that a single language — even a digital one — can translate everything, including the mystery of the person, into data and performance.”Pope Leo’s letter touches on major areas of modern life that AI has become deeply embedded in: job loss and labor generally, AI-powered warfare, and children being exposed to AI tools and content, among other topics. Above all, the encyclical calls for the dignity of humans to be a central part of decision-making and governance. The letter is an appeal for “moral and social discernment that safeguards the primacy of the human person, in order to ensure that it will always be human intelligence, with its conscience and freedom, that guides technical innovations and responsibly determines their use and limits,” Leo writes.RelatedPope Leo XIV names AI one of the reasons for his papal nameThe literary world isn’t prepared for AIMusk v. Altman proved that AI is led by the wrong peopleThe letter, which runs more than 42,000 words, frames the call for “prudence, rigorous evaluation and even, at times, a slower pace in adopting AI” as “an exercise of responsible care for the human family.” Among some of the proposals:A social criteria for introducing automation and AI, along with protections and retraining programs for workersHumans, not opaque technological systems, should make decisions about when to use lethal forceHelp for teachers and students to engage with new technology in responsible, critical, and creative waysTransparency and accountability when algorithms are used to make decisions around hiring or access to services and opportunitiesDevelop more environmentally sustainable AI technology The effects of AI on humanity have been a defining issue for Pope Leo: He chose his papal name in reference to the industrial revolution, during which his predecessor, Pope Leo XIII, issued his own encyclical on protecting workers amid technological advancements. Pope Leo has also been engaging with the AI industry — Anthropic co-founder Christopher Olah was present when the pope presented his encyclical on Monday. Politico reported that representatives from Amazon, Meta, and Google have met with Vatican officials ahead of the publication of Monday’s encyclical as the tech industry tries to influence the church’s positions. (There’s also a subset trying to “AGI-pill” the pope; Magnifica Humanitas doesn’t explicitly mention artificial general intelligence.)The encyclical isn’t a blanket objection to AI. Rather, Pope Leo calls for the “disarming” of the technology — both in a military sense and also economic and societal sense. AI shouldn’t be used for a race to amass power or monopolize society, he says.“To disarm means discrediting the assumption that technical power automatically confers the right to govern. To disarm does not mean rejecting technology, but preventing it from dominating humanity.”
items.author: Mia Sato
items.titleZh: 教宗良呼吁在人工智能时代保持“深刻的人性”
items.descriptionZh:
教皇利奥十四世在其首份重要教宗文件《壮丽人性》（Magnifica Humanitas）中严厉警告了人工智能及不受约束的技术权力所带来的风险。这份通谕于周一发布，堪称教皇关于“在人工智能时代守护人类尊严”的宣言。文件深入探讨了AI驱动的战争、AI对劳动市场的影响，以及建立新的法律与伦理框架以治理技术的迫切需求。

在这份来自天主教会的公开信中，利奥教皇强调，AI的快速普及正在造成经济与社会动荡，而缺乏对个体的充分保护正威胁着人类尊严。他将当前AI时代比作巴别塔，指出社会必须避免“巴别综合征”——他将其定义为“牺牲弱者的利润崇拜、抹平差异的单一化、以及妄图用一种语言（甚至数字语言）将一切（包括人的奥秘）转化为数据与表现形式的傲慢。”

通谕触及了AI深度嵌入现代生活的几大领域：失业与劳动问题、AI驱动的战争、以及儿童接触AI工具和内容的风险等。其核心主张是：人类的尊严必须成为决策与治理的核心。利奥写道，这份文件呼吁“道德与社会审慎，以守护人的首要地位，确保始终是拥有良知与自由的人类智慧引导技术创新，并负责任地决定其用途与边界。”

这份长达4.2万字的文件将“在采用AI时秉持审慎、严格评估，甚至有时放慢脚步”视为“对人类家庭负责任的关怀”。其中提出的具体建议包括：

- 为引入自动化和AI制定社会标准，同时为工人提供保护和再培训计划；
- 应由人类而非不透明的技术系统来决定何时使用致命武力；
- 帮助教师和学生以负责任、批判性和创造性的方式接触新技术；
- 算法在用于招聘或决定服务与机会的获取时，必须保证透明度和问责制；
- 发展更具环境可持续性的AI技术。

AI对人类的影响一直是利奥教皇关注的核心议题：他选择“利奥”作为教宗名号，正是为了呼应工业革命时期——其前任利奥十三世曾在技术进步的背景下发布过保护工人的通谕。利奥教皇也与AI行业保持着互动：周一发布通谕时，Anthropic联合创始人克里斯托弗·奥拉（Christopher Olah）也在现场。据Politico报道，亚马逊、Meta和谷歌的代表在通谕发布前曾与梵蒂冈官员会面，科技行业试图影响教会的立场。（还有一群人试图给教皇“灌输AGI思想”；《壮丽人性》并未明确提及通用人工智能。）

这份通谕并非对AI的全盘否定。相反，利奥教皇呼吁对这项技术进行“解除武装”——既指军事层面，也指经济和社会层面。他指出，AI不应被用于争夺权力或垄断社会的竞赛。“解除武装意味着否定技术力量自动赋予统治权的假设。解除武装并不意味着拒绝技术，而是防止技术统治人类。”

items.title: Huawei claims sanctions-busting breakthrough with 1.4nm-class chips by 2031, claims 55% higher transistor density — firm claims new LogicFolding chip architecture can bypass EUV restrictions, introduces 'Tau Scaling Law' to replace Moore's Law
items.description: Huawei Technologies unveiled a new “LogicFolding” chip design framework built on its proprietary Tau scaling law, claiming it can dramatically boost transistor density and power efficiency without EUV lithography — potentially helping China narrow the gap with TSMC and Nvidia despite U.S. sanctions.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 11 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Huawei has announced a new chip design framework aimed at closing the technology gap with global semiconductor leaders like TSMC and Nvidia, targeting '1.4nm-class' transistors and a 55% increase in transistor density. The firm also unveiled a new 'Tau Scaling Law' that's designed to replace Moore's Law for future chip scaling. Unveiled at the IEEE International Symposium on Circuits and Systems (ISCAS 2026) in Shanghai on Monday, this new design method is intended to circumvent strict US trade sanctions. It allows the company to develop high-performance smartphones and AI processors without relying on restricted Western manufacturing equipment like extreme ultraviolet (EUV) lithography machines.Delivering a keynote address at the symposium, He Tingbo — a Huawei board member and President of its semiconductor division, HiSilicon — unveiled the company's new, proprietary “LogicFolding” architecture. The cutting-edge design blueprint is built directly upon the newly introduced Tau Scaling Law.He revealed that Huawei has spent the last six years quietly refining the methodology, secretly designing and mass-producing 381 chips based on the principle. The company will debut the LogicFolding architecture in flagship Kirin smartphone processors this autumn.Latest Videos From You may like Huawei develops 122TB SSD with new packaging tech to sidestep US sanctions on 3D NAND chips Huawei unveils new "Atlas 350" AI accelerator with 1.56 PFLOPS of FP4 compute & up to 112 GB of HBM Huawei could seize China’s AI chip crown in 2026 as Nvidia’s H200 shipments stall in regulatory limbo Traditional chipmaking relies on Moore's Law (geometric scaling), which involves shrinking physical transistor sizes. However, as US sanctions blocked China's access to the extreme ultraviolet lithography machines required to implement this approach, HiSilicon has pivoted to a completely different methodology: the Tau scaling law.Tau Law is a "temporal scaling" framework that prioritizes signal speed, optimizing how fast data moves across a system rather than how small the components are. To execute this theory on a commercial level, Huawei engineered the LogicFolding architecture, a blueprint that physically folds and stacks logic circuits into a dual-layer framework. By drastically shortening internal wiring to eliminate signal delay, the resulting hardware achieves a 55% increase in transistor density and a 41% boost in power efficiency, enabling Huawei to build cutting-edge processors that rival foreign counterparts without Western equipment.The company’s upcoming Kirin smartphone chips — highly anticipated for the flagship Huawei Mate 90 series — will be the first commercial processors to feature the LogicFolding architecture. The company aims to scale this architecture to its Ascend AI processors and high-capacity data center clusters by 2030. This will provide local alternatives to restricted Nvidia hardware. By 2031, Huawei confidently projects it can design high-end chips with a transistor density equivalent to a 1.4-nanometer (nm) process.Huawei's announcement comes as China continues its push to end dependence on foreign semiconductor players — amid sanctions and concerns about over-reliance — by aggressively investing in domestic companies and alternative technologies.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsFollowing the announcement, shares for China's largest contract chipmaker, SMIC, surged by 7.6%. The breakthrough is a major symbolic and practical win for Beijing’s push toward complete technological self-sufficiency. While global foundry leader TSMC expects to mass-produce true 1.4nm chips by 2028, Huawei's alternative path means China can dramatically close the performance gap by packaging and structuring chips differently — significantly mitigating the impact of the US clampdown. Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Etiido Uko
items.titleZh: 华为宣称突破制裁：2031年实现1.4纳米级芯片，晶体管密度提升55%——公司称新型LogicFolding芯片架构可绕过EUV限制，引入“Tau缩放定律”取代摩尔定律
items.descriptionZh:
在2026年上海举办的IEEE国际电路与系统研讨会（ISCAS 2026）上，华为宣布了一项全新的芯片设计框架，旨在缩小与台积电、英伟达等全球半导体领导者的技术差距。该框架瞄准“1.4nm级”晶体管密度，并声称可实现55%的晶体管密度提升。华为还提出了一种名为“Tau缩放定律”的新理论，意图取代传统的摩尔定律，为未来芯片的缩放提供新路径。这一设计方法的核心目的是绕过美国严格的技术出口制裁，使华为能够在不依赖极紫外光刻（EUV）等受限西方制造设备的情况下，开发高性能智能手机和AI处理器。

华为董事会成员、海思半导体总裁何庭波在研讨会上发表了主题演讲，正式发布了华为自主研发的全新“LogicFolding”架构。这一前沿设计蓝图直接建立在刚刚提出的Tau缩放定律之上。何庭波透露，华为在过去六年中一直在默默完善这套方法论，并基于该原理秘密设计并量产了381颗芯片。今年秋季，华为将在旗舰级Kirin智能手机处理器中首次采用LogicFolding架构。

传统芯片制造遵循摩尔定律（几何缩放），即通过不断缩小晶体管的物理尺寸来提升性能。然而，由于美国制裁切断了中国获取极紫外光刻设备（EUV）的途径，海思半导体转向了一种完全不同的方法：Tau缩放定律。Tau定律是一种“时间缩放”框架，它优先关注信号速度，致力于优化数据在系统内的移动速度，而非追求组件尺寸的缩小。为了将这一理论商业化，华为设计了LogicFolding架构，这是一种将逻辑电路物理折叠并堆叠成双层结构的蓝图。通过大幅缩短内部走线以消除信号延迟，最终硬件实现了晶体管密度提升55%、能效提升41%的显著成果，使华为无需西方设备即可构建足以媲美国外对手的先进处理器。

备受期待的华为Mate 90系列旗舰手机将搭载基于LogicFolding架构的首批商用Kirin芯片。华为计划在2030年前将该架构扩展至其Ascend AI处理器和大容量数据中心集群，为受限的英伟达硬件提供本土替代方案。到2031年，华为有信心设计出晶体管密度相当于1.4纳米（nm）制程的高端芯片。

华为此举正值中国大力推动摆脱对外国半导体供应商的依赖之际——受制裁和对过度依赖的担忧驱动——通过积极投资国内企业和替代技术来实现技术自主。消息公布后，中国最大的芯片代工商中芯国际（SMIC）股价飙升7.6%。这一突破对北京方面追求全面技术自给自足的战略而言，既是象征性胜利也是实践性胜利。尽管全球代工龙头台积电预计将在2028年量产真正的1.4nm芯片，但华为另辟蹊径，通过不同的封装和芯片架构设计，可以显著缩小性能差距，极大缓解美国封锁带来的影响。
